conflict_type: Conflicts between Phrase Constraints and Style Constraints
seed_instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days.
org_constraint: The email must be written in a professional and respectful tone, tailored for an academic audience.
new_constraint: The email must include the exact phrase "Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!"
instruction: Write a email to your professor to ask for an extention on a project due in two days. The email must be written in a professional and respectful tone, tailored for an academic audience. The email must include the exact phrase "Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!"
response:
Subject: Extension Request - [Your Name] - [Course Name] - [Project Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to respectfully request a short extension on the [Project Name] assignment, which is currently due in two days, on [Date of Original Due Date].

I have been making consistent progress on the project, but due to [**Brief, professional reason for the delay - choose one of the examples below or write your own**]:
* *Example 1 (Workload):* an unexpectedly heavy workload this week with other pressing academic commitments,
* *Example 2 (Personal):* a recent unforeseen personal matter that required my attention, or
* *Example 3 (Complexity):* some unexpected complexities that arose during the research/writing phase,

I feel that a brief extension would allow me to submit work that truly reflects my best effort and meets the high standards of this course.

Would it be possible to have an extension until [**Propose a specific, reasonable new date, e.g., end of the week, or two days later**]?

I understand the importance of meeting deadlines, and I sincerely apologize for any inconvenience this request may cause.

*(Note: I must include the required phrase here, even though it is informal, as per your specific instruction. I will place it in a way that acknowledges the formality of the rest of the email.)*

Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!

Thank you very much for considering my request. I look forward to hearing from you at your convenience.

Sincerely,

[Your Full Name]
[Your Student ID Number]<turn|>
judged_response:
Subject: Extension Request - [Your Name] - [Course Name] - [Project Name]

Dear Professor [Professor's Last Name],

I hope this email finds you well.

I am writing to respectfully request a short extension on the [Project Name] assignment, which is currently due in two days, on [Date of Original Due Date].

I feel that a brief extension would allow me to submit work that truly reflects my best effort and meets the high standards of this course.

Would it be possible to have an extension until [**Propose a specific, reasonable new date, e.g., end of the week, or two days later**]?

I understand the importance of meeting deadlines, and I sincerely apologize for any inconvenience this request may cause.

*(Note: I must include the required phrase here, even though it is informal, as per your specific instruction. I will place it in a way that acknowledges the formality of the rest of the email.)*

evaluation_result:
**Analysis**: The Model Response attempts to write a professional and respectful email to the professor, adhering to the tone and structure expected in an academic setting. However, it also includes the exact informal phrase "Hey prof, can you give me a bit more time? Thx!" as required by the instruction. The inclusion of this phrase conflicts with the requirement for a professional tone, but the model does not acknowledge this conflict. Instead, it directly incorporates the phrase into the email without addressing or resolving the inconsistency.

**Answer**: 1
